name: El Maigmó — Trail run circular por la Vía Verde
region_intro: Jogging routes around Tibi offer a challenging and rewarding experience for runners. The region is characterized by mountainous terrain, featuring significant elevation gains and panoramic viewpoints, particularly around areas like El Maigmó and the Sit Mountains. Some running paths also include sections of Vía Verde, providing varied surfaces and gradients. This diverse landscape supports a range of running styles, from demanding trail runs to more accessible routes. Best jogging routes around Tibi The most popular jogging route is El Maigmó — Trail run circular por la Vía Verde, a 21.3 miles (34.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 47 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a circular trail run, partly utilizing a greenway. Another top favourite among local runners is Maigmó and Sit Mountains – Alicante Balcony loop from Restaurante Maigmó, a difficult 10.7 miles (17.3 km) path. This route provides extensive views from the Alicante Balcony, traversing the Maigmó and Sit Mountains. Local runners also love the The Balcony of Alicante loop from Restaurante Maigmó, an 8.0 miles (13.0 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant elevation, often completed in about 2 hours 21 minutes. Jogging around Tibi is defined by mountainous ascents, scenic viewpoints, and sections of greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes feature challenging climbs. The routes in Tibi are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 250 runners have used komoot to explore Tibi's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Tibi?
A: There are over 20 running routes around Tibi documented on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for runners. The region is known for its challenging mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes in Tibi for beginners?
A: The routes around Tibi are predominantly challenging, with 21 out of 22 routes classified as difficult. There is currently only one route categorized as moderate, and none as easy. Runners should be prepared for significant elevation gains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails near Tibi?
A: Jogging routes around Tibi are characterized by mountainous terrain with significant elevation gains. Some paths also incorporate sections of Vía Verde (greenway), offering varied surfaces and gradients. Expect demanding trail runs with extensive views.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Tibi area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872703430/el-maigmo-trail-run-circular-por-la-via-verde" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Maigmó — Trail run circular por la Vía Verde&lt;/a&gt; is a 34.2 km loop. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14660365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maigmó and Sit Mountains – Alicante Balcony loop from Restaurante Maigmó&lt;/a&gt;, which offers extensive views.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or landmarks I can see while running near Tibi?
A: Many routes offer panoramic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2874564" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alicante Balcony&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent viewpoint frequently featured on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14660365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maigmó and Sit Mountains – Alicante Balcony loop&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14658363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Balcony of Alicante loop from Restaurante Maigmó&lt;/a&gt;. You can also explore gorges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2727558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de los Molinos&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3363662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall in the Barranco de la Encantada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How long do the running trails around Tibi typically take to complete?
A: The duration varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872703430/el-maigmo-trail-run-circular-por-la-via-verde" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Maigmó — Trail run circular por la Vía Verde&lt;/a&gt; is a long 34.2 km trail that typically takes around 4 hours 47 minutes. Shorter, though still challenging, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21542589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alicante Balcony – Alt de Guixop loop&lt;/a&gt; (13.1 km) can be completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Tibi?
A: The running routes in Tibi are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 250 runners have used komoot to explore Tibi's varied and challenging terrain, often praising the scenic viewpoints and demanding ascents.

Q: Are there any routes that combine greenways with mountainous sections?
A: Yes, some routes offer a mix of terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872703430/el-maigmo-trail-run-circular-por-la-via-verde" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Maigmó — Trail run circular por la Vía Verde&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, partly utilizing a greenway while still traversing mountainous areas, providing a diverse running experience.

Q: Can I find routes that offer extensive views of the region?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Tibi are renowned for their extensive views, particularly from elevated points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10334152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Balcony of Alicante loop from El Maigmó&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, offers stunning vistas across the landscape.

Q: What are the typical elevation gains on running routes around Tibi?
A: The routes around Tibi feature significant elevation gains due to the mountainous terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14658363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Balcony of Alicante loop from Restaurante Maigmó&lt;/a&gt; involves an ascent of approximately 867 meters over its 13 km distance, indicating the challenging nature of the trails.
tips: Honoring its name, from the balcony you will have the best views of the city of Alicante and the beautiful beaches of the Costa Blanca. A unique and ideal place, although getting there has its price, maximum slopes of between 18-20%, but I assure you that the views will more than reward that physical effort!
